title: Bulliarda moschata
intro: Bulliarda moschata, also known as musk-root or musk-wort, is a plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. The health benefits of Bulliarda moschata include its traditional use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, particularly gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Bulliarda moschata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or powder to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Bulliarda moschata is classified as a species within the genus Bulliarda and the family Asclepiadaceae, which is now considered a synonym of Apocynaceae. This classification places it within the order Gentianales. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. The genus Bulliarda is characterized by its unique flowers and is primarily found in tropical regions. The classification of Bulliarda moschata reflects its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
